title: rubus idaeus f. marmoratus
intro: Rubus idaeus f. marmoratus, also known as the marble-leaved raspberry, is a subspecies of the red raspberry plant. It is a type of shrub that belongs to the rose family and is native to Europe and Western Asia. The leaves of Rubus idaeus f. marmoratus have been used in herbal medicine for their astringent and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the leaves can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome.
